title: Stock market today: Nasdaq, S&P 500 jump as Big Tech earnings, trade deals lift spirits Wall Street weighed a new trade deal with South Korea and strong earnings from Meta and Microsoft.

Knit Secures $16.1M Series A to Redefine Enterprise Insights with Researcher-Driven AI Knit, the AI-powered platform transforming how enterprises conduct consumer research, today announced it has raised $16.1 million in Series A funding. The round was led by GFT Ventures and Ashton Kutcher's Sound Ventures with continued participation from Silicon Road Ventures, Osage Ventures, Rise of the Rest, and others. Notable angel investors such as David Kirk, former Chief Scientist at NVIDIA, and Gokul Rajaram, founding partner of Marathon Management, also participated in this round.

E-Commerce Today - Tackling Rising Fraud With Advanced Security Measures E-commerce and online marketplaces are facing soaring rates of fraud, significantly impacting revenues and customer trust. According to Veriff's recent "The Future of E-commerce" report, e-commerce is experiencing the highest level of authorized fraud scams across all industries, with incidents spiking during peak shopping periods like Black Friday and Cyber Monday. The report emphasizes the necessity of robust identity verification, highlighting that 75% of consumers consider this a crucial...

CodeSignal to Launch Free Generative AI Training Program on AWS to Upskill 30,000+ Students Globally CodeSignal, a leading skills assessment and experiential learning platform, today announced an expanded skilling program powered by Amazon Web Services (AWS). It will offer new generative AI learning paths at no cost to AWS Skills to Jobs Tech Alliance members, including over 30,000 students and 1,000 faculty globally, and to millions of learners through CodeSignal. AWS Skills to Jobs Tech Alliance is a global coalition that addresses the skills gap and prepares learners for in-demand tech jobs.

India's Swiggy reports wider quarterly loss on marketing cost spike Swiggy, a decade-old player and one of the market leaders in the food delivery business alongside Eternal’s Zomato, continues investing in the business through marketing, platform upgrades and loyalty programs.  The company faced challenges related to lower availability of delivery partners due to earlier-than-expected monsoon showers in India.  Meanwhile, marketing investments remained high amid "sticky competitive intensity," it said in a statement.
